topic: batonnage, lees contact, texture, and complexity

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Fine lees settle after fermentation and remain available for contact with a white wine.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Batonnage resuspends the fine lees, increasing contact between the wine and yeast-derived mannoproteins, polysaccharides, and flavour compounds.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine can gain a creamier texture, greater palate weight, and more aromatic complexity.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The wine can gain a creamier texture, greater palate weight, and more aromatic complexity.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
